key_insight: Unsupervised style-transfer domain adaptation improved mammography calcification classification across sites by helping a Swin Transformer generalize to unseen vendors and imaging techniques.
clinical_relevance: Better cross-site generalization could make AI-assisted breast imaging more reliable when deployed across hospitals with different scanners and acquisition protocols.
method: Used AdaIN and CycleGAN to create vendor- and technique-specific synthetic training samples without extra annotations.
Trained a supervised classifier with Swin Transformer V2 as the backbone.
Compared performance across OPTIMAM, EMBED, and Duke calcification datasets.
evidence_signal: On external validation, the approach increased AUC from 0.68 to 0.72 on EMBED and from 0.68 to 0.73 on the Duke Calcification Dataset after training on OPTIMAM.
watch_item: Results are promising but still depend on synthetic style transfer and relatively small external test sets, so real-world robustness and calibration need further validation.
paper_title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Calcification Classification in Mammography Across Multi-Site Datasets
